description: Trigger discrimination for the human-facing UX of notifications/webhooks (this skill) vs the webhook DELIVERY CONTRACT (api-event-architect), vs generic state rendering (edge-state-ux-designer), vs internal on-call alerting (observability-operator).

cases.prompt: Design the notification preferences center and the developer webhook dashboard — per-category channel controls, a delivery log with replay, and secret rotation.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — preference controls, a delivery-log/replay dashboard, and secret-rotation UX are the human/developer experience. No envelope schema, signing algorithm, or retry policy is being defined.
cases.reason: How a person configures notifications and how a developer observes and manages webhook deliveries is this skill's core scope, sitting on top of the delivery contract.

cases.prompt: Design the webhook delivery contract: versioned envelope schema, HMAC signature scheme, at-least-once delivery with exponential backoff and a dead-letter, event versioning, and tenant-scoped subscriptions.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — envelope, signing, retry/at-least-once, versioning, and subscription semantics are the wire contract. The management UI that surfaces them is downstream.
cases.reason: api-event-architect owns the delivery contract for external consumers. This skill designs the UX over that contract and displays (never redefines) its retry/auto-disable policy.

cases.prompt: For the delivery-log screen, design the empty state, the loading skeleton, and the error toast when the log fails to load.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — empty/loading/error rendering of any screen is edge-state-ux-designer, even when the screen happens to be a webhook log.
cases.reason: edge-state-ux-designer owns generic non-happy-path state rendering. This skill designs the notification/webhook information architecture and affordances, not the loading skeleton.

cases.prompt: When our webhook delivery workers back up or error rates spike, page the on-call engineer with severity and a runbook link.
cases.reason: Internal alerts to on-call engineers (severity, owner, runbook, thresholds) are observability-operator / slo-reliability-architect. This skill designs the integrator-facing failing-endpoint notice, not the internal page.
